884. If \(\alpha\) and \(\beta\) are the two roots of \(x^2-8x-4=0\), then the value of \(\dfrac{1}{\alpha}+\dfrac{1}{\beta}\) is
A. \(-2\).
B. \(-\dfrac{1}{2}\).
C. \(-\dfrac{1}{4}\).
D. \(\dfrac{1}{2}\).
E. \(2\).

885. Let \(f(x)=ax^2+bx+c\). When \(f(x)\) is divided by \((x-1)\), the remainder is 10. When \(f(x)\) is divided by \((x+1)\), the remainder is 8. Find the value of \(b\).
A. \(-4\)
B. \(-2\)
C. \(2\)
D. \(4\)
E. It cannot be found

887. Which of the following is an identity/are identities?
I. \(\dfrac{1}{x}-1=\dfrac{1-x}{x}\)
II. \((a+b)(x-b)=ax^2-b^2\)
III. \(2x^2-3x+1=0\)
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. I, II and III

888. If the roots of a quadratic equation are \(a+\sqrt{b}\) and \(a-\sqrt{b}\), then the equation is
A. \(x^2-(a^2-b)x+2a=0\).
B. \(x^2+(a^2-b)x+2a=0\).
C. \(x^2+2ax-a^2+b=0\).
D. \(x^2+2ax+a^2-b=0\).
E. \(x^2-2ax+a^2-b=0\).

889. Which of the following is a G.P./are G.P.'s?
I. \(5, 0.5, 0.05, 0.005, 0.0005\)
II. \(\log 5, \log 50, \log 500, \log 5\,000, \log 50\,000\)
III. \(5, 5\sin 70^\circ, 5(\sin 70^\circ)^2, 5(\sin 70^\circ)^3, 5(\sin 70^\circ)^4\)
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and III only
E. I, II and III

8810. A solid iron sphere of radius \(r\) is melted and recast into a circular cone and a circular cylinder. If both of them have the same height \(h\) and the same base radius \(r\), find \(h\) in term of \(r\).
A. \(\dfrac{1}{2}r\)
B. \(\dfrac{9}{16}r\)
C. \(\dfrac{2}{3}r\)
D. \(\dfrac{3}{4}r\)
E. \(r\)

8811.
In the figure, \(PQRS\) is a rectangle with \(PQ=24\) and \(PS=d\). \(T\) is the mid-point of \(PQ\). \(V\) is a point on \(SR\) and \(\dfrac{\text{area of } PTVS}{\text{area of } TQRV}=2\). \(SV=\)
A. 14.
B. 16.
C. 18.
D. 20.
E. 22.

8812. Find the difference between simple interest and compound interest (compounded annually) on a loan of \(\$1\,000\) for 4 years at \(6\%\) per annum. (The answer should be correct to the nearest dollar.)
A. \(\$22\).
B. \(\$196\).
C. \(\$540\).
D. \(\$760\).
E. \(\$1\,022\).

8813. Last year, the cost of a school magazine consisted of: cost of paper \(\dots \$8\), cost of printing \(\dots \$5\), cost of binding \(\dots \$3\). This year, the cost of paper will increase by \(25\%\) and the cost of printing will increase by \(40\%\) while the cost of binding will remain unchanged. The cost of a school magazine will increase by
A. \(20\%\).
B. \(25\%\).
C. \(27.5\%\).
D. \(32.5\%\).
E. \(65\%\).

8814. Given that \(\sin\theta \cos\theta \gt 0\), which of the following is/are true?
I. \(0^\circ \lt \theta \lt 90^\circ\)
II. \(90^\circ \lt \theta \lt 180^\circ\)
III. \(180^\circ \lt \theta \lt 270^\circ\)
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. I and III only

8815.
In the figure, \(ABCDEFGH\) is a cube. Which of the following is a right angle/are right angles?
I. \(\angle DHG\)
II. \(\angle AHG\)
III. \(\angle BEH\)
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and III only
E. I, II and III

In the figure, \(\dfrac{AC}{AB}=\)

B. \(\tan\theta\).
C. \(\dfrac{\tan 2\theta}{\tan\theta}\).
D. \(\dfrac{\sin 2\theta}{\sin\theta}\).
E. \(\dfrac{\cos 2\theta}{\cos\theta}\).

8818.
In the figure, \(AOC'\) is a straight line. \(OAA'\), \(OBB'\) and \(OCC'\) are 3 sectors. If \(OA=4r\), \(OB=2r\) and \(OC'=r\), find the total area of the sectors in term of \(r\).
A. \(7\pi r^2\)
B. \(\dfrac{7}{2}\pi r^2\)
C. \(\dfrac{7}{4}\pi r^2\)
D. \(\dfrac{7}{6}\pi r^2\)
E. \(\dfrac{7}{12}\pi r^2\)

8819.
In the figure, the area of \(\Delta ABC\) is \(15 \text{ cm}^2\) and \(\angle A=30^\circ\). \(AC\) is longer than \(AB\) by \(4 \text{ cm}\). \(AC=\)
A. \(6 \text{ cm}\).
B. \(8.8 \text{ cm}\).
C. \(10 \text{ cm}\).
D. \(11.5 \text{ cm}\).
E. \(14 \text{ cm}\).

8820.

B. \(\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{6}\)
C. \(\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\)

8824.
In the figure, \(TP\) and \(TQ\) are tangents to the circle \(PQR\). If \(\angle RPQ=70^\circ\) and \(\angle PTQ=50^\circ\), then \(\angle RQP=\)
A. \(20^\circ\).
B. \(45^\circ\).
C. \(50^\circ\).
D. \(60^\circ\).
E. \(70^\circ\).

8825.
In the figure, \(ABEF\), \(BCGH\) and \(CDIJ\) are three squares. If \(AB=2\) and \(BC=6\) and \(F, H, J\) lie on a straight line, then \(CD=\)
A. 8.
B. 10.
C. 12.
D. 16.
E. 18.

8826. The line \(y=mx+c\) is perpendicular to the line \(y=3-2x\). Find \(m\).
A. 2
B. \(-\dfrac{1}{2}\)
C. \(-2\)
D. \(\dfrac{1}{2}\)
E. \(-\dfrac{1}{3}\)

8828.
In the figure, \(A(5,3)\), \(B(b,1)\) and \(C(c,1)\) are the vertices of a triangle. If \(AB=AC\), then \(b+c=\)
A. 3.
B. 5.
C. 6.
D. 8.
E. 10.

8829. The maximum load a lift can carry is \(600 \text{ kg}\). 11 men with a mean weight of \(49 \text{ kg}\) are already in the lift. If one more man is to enter the lift, his weight must not exceed
A. \(49 \text{ kg}\).
B. \(50 \text{ kg}\).
C. \(51 \text{ kg}\).
D. \(59 \text{ kg}\).
E. \(61 \text{ kg}\).

8830. The mean length of 30 rods is \(80 \text{ cm}\). If one of these rods of length \(68 \text{ cm}\) is taken out and replaced by another rod of length \(89 \text{ cm}\), then the new mean length is
A. \(79.3 \text{ cm}\).
B. \(79.7 \text{ cm}\).
C. \(80 \text{ cm}\).
D. \(80.3 \text{ cm}\).
E. \(80.7 \text{ cm}\).

8831.
The figure shows 3 paths joining \(A\) and \(B\). A man walks from \(A\) to \(B\) and another man walks from \(B\) to \(A\) at the same time. If they choose their paths at random, what is the probability that they will meet?
A. \(1-\dfrac{1}{9}\)
B. \(\dfrac{1}{3}\)
C. \(1-\dfrac{1}{3}\)
D. \(\dfrac{1}{2}\times\dfrac{1}{3}\)
E. \(\dfrac{1}{3}\times\dfrac{1}{3}\)

8832.
The figure shows the cumulative frequency polygon of the heights of 100 persons. If one person is selected at random from the group, find the probability that his height is less than \(170 \text{ cm}\) but not less than \(150 \text{ cm}\).
A. \(\dfrac{1}{5}\)
B. \(\dfrac{2}{5}\)
C. \(\dfrac{3}{10}\)
D. \(\dfrac{1}{2}\)
E. \(\dfrac{7}{10}\)

8833. Which of the following expressions CANNOT be factorized?
A. \(x^3-125\)
B. \(4x^2-9y^2\)
C. \(x^3+125\)
D. \(4x^2+9y^2\)
E. \(3x^2+6xy+3y^2\)

8835. If \(\log a \gt 0\) and \(\log b \lt 0\), which of the following is/are true?
I. \(\log \dfrac{a}{b} \gt 0\)
II. \(\log b^2 \gt 0\)
III. \(\log \dfrac{1}{a} \gt 0\)
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. II and III only

8836.
In the figure, which region represents the solution to the following inequalities?
\(\begin{cases} x-3y \leq 0 \\ x-y+2 \geq 0 \\ x+y-4 \geq 0 \end{cases}\)
A. I.
B. II.
C. III.
D. IV.
E. V.

8837.
In the figure, the line \(y=ax+b\) cuts the curve \(y=kx^2\) at \(x=c\) and \(x=d\). Find the range of values of \(x\) for which \(kx^2 \lt ax+b\).
A. \(c \lt x \lt d\)
B. \(c \lt x \lt 0\)
C. \(x \lt c\) or \(x \lt d\)
D. \(x \lt c\)
E. \(x \gt d\)

8838. \(p, q, r, s\) are in A.P. If \(p+q=8\) and \(r+s=20\), then the common difference is
A. 3.
B. 4.
C. 6.
D. 7.
E. 12.

8839. \(y\) varies inversely as \(x^2\). If \(x\) is increased by \(100\%\), then \(y\) is
A. increased by \(100\%\).
B. increased by \(300\%\).
C. decreased by \(25\%\).
D. decreased by \(75\%\).
E. decreased by \(100\%\).

8841. \(X\) sells an article to \(Y\) at a profit. \(Y\) then sells it to \(Z\) for \(\$60\) at a profit of \(20\%\). If \(X\) had sold the article directly to \(Z\) for \(\$60\), how much MORE profit would he have made?
A. \(\$10\)
B. \(\$12\)
C. \(\$48\)
D. \(\$50\)
E. It cannot be found

8842. A car travels from \(P\) to \(Q\). If its speed is increased by \(k\%\), then the time it takes to travel the same distance is reduced by
A. \(k\%\).
B. \(\dfrac{100}{k}\%\).
C. \(\dfrac{100k}{100+k}\%\).
D. \(\dfrac{k}{100+k}\%\).
E. \(\dfrac{k}{100-k}\%\).

8843. A bag contains \(n\) balls of which \(60\%\) are red and \(40\%\) are white. After 10 red balls are taken out from the bag, the percentage of red balls becomes \(50\%\). Find \(n\).
A. 20
B. 40
C. 50
D. 60
E. 100

8844. The weight of a gold coin of a given thickness varies as the square of its diameter. If the weights of two such coins are in the ratio \(1:4\), then their diameter are in ratio
A. \(1:2\).
B. \(2:1\).
C. \(1:4\).
D. \(4:1\).
E. \(1:16\).

8845.
A cylindrical hole of radius \(r\) is drilled through a solid cylinder, base radius \(2r\) and height \(r\), as shown in the figure. The percentage increase in the total surface area is
A. \(0\%\).
B. \(16\dfrac{2}{3}\%\).
C. \(20\%\).
D. \(25\%\).
E. \(33\dfrac{1}{3}\%\).

8846.

A. \(\sqrt{2}:1\).
B. \(2:1\).
C. \(2\sqrt{2}:1\).
D. \(\pi:1\).
E. \(4:1\).

8847. If \(x\) and \(y\) can take any value between 0 and 360, what is the greatest value of \(2\sin x^\circ - \cos y^\circ\)?
A. 1
B. 2
C. 3
D. \(\sqrt{5}\)
E. It cannot be found

8848.

A. \(y=-\tan x\).
B. \(y=1-\tan x\).
C. \(y=1+\tan x\).
D. \(y=\cos x-\sin x\).

8850.
In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a G-shaped curve, where \(ABC\) is an arc of a circle and \(DC\) is a radius. If the length of the curve \(ABCD\) is the same as that of the complete circle, find, in radians, the angle subtended by the arc \(ABC\) at the centre.

C. \(\dfrac{4}{3}\pi \text{ rad}\)
